channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The Venice Biennale jury resigns amid tensions over awards ban, Russian participation
item.description: The international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned Thursday amid tensions over Russia's participation and the panel's decision to bar prizes for countries accused of crimes against humanity.
item.content:encoded:
The international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned Thursday amid tensions over Russia's participation and the panel's decision to bar prizes for countries accused of crimes against humanity.

(Image credit: Antonio Calanni)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Zelenskyy says he's seeking details of Putin's May 9 ceasefire proposal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is seeking details of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is seeking details of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to U.S. President Donald Trump.

(Image credit: Petros Karadjias)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 20,000 civilian sailors are stuck in the gulf that opens to the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Thousands of seafarers remain stuck as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues. The United Nations urges for a plan to facilitate their release.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of seafarers remain stuck as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues. The United Nations urges for a plan to facilitate their release.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Myanmar junta says Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, doubts linger
item.description: The secrecy surrounding Suu Kyi's new location has alarmed her son, Kim Aris, who says he has received no authoritative confirmation of her wellbeing.
item.content:encoded: The secrecy surrounding Suu Kyi's new location has alarmed her son, Kim Aris, who says he has received no authoritative confirmation of her wellbeing.
item.dc:creator: Lorcan Lovett

item.title: U.K. calls antisemitism an emergency after arson and stabbing attacks on Jewish people
item.description: The British government pledged to increase security for Jewish communities after a string of arson attacks and a double stabbing. But members of the community lashed out at the government.
item.content:encoded:
The British government pledged to increase security for Jewish communities after a string of arson attacks and a double stabbing. But members of the community lashed out at the government.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
item.description: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
item.description: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.
item.content:encoded:
In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.

(Image credit: Ariel Schalit)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
item.description: Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.
item.content:encoded:
Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.

(Image credit: Joan Mateu Parra)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing American troop presence in Germany after Iran feud
item.description: President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
